1502			Long: `execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
1503	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1504	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1505	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.

1507	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1508	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1509	
1510	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1511	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.

1513	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1514	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1515	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)

1523	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1524	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1525	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1526	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.

922	func (m *WorkerManager) List() ([]WorkerRecord, error) {
923		if err := os.MkdirAll(m.rootDir, 0o755); err != nil {
924			return nil, err
925		}
926		entries, err := os.ReadDir(m.rootDir)
927		if err != nil {
928			return nil, err
929		}
930		var out []WorkerRecord
931		for _, entry := range entries {
932			if !entry.IsDir() {
933				continue
934			}
935			rec, err := m.readRecord(filepath.Join(m.rootDir, entry.Name()))
936			if err != nil {
937				continue
938			}
939			out = append(out, rec)
940		}
941	
942		// Merge in-memory state for active workers
943		m.mu.Lock()
944		for i := range out {
945			if handle, ok := m.workers[out[i].ID]; ok {
946				out[i] = handle.record
947			}
948		}
949		m.mu.Unlock()
950	
951		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool {
952			return out[i].StartedAt.After(out[j].StartedAt)
953		})
954	
955		// Compute PIDAlive for each record so operators can see liveness at a
956		// glance without running prune. This is a syscall per record; it's safe
957		// because List() is not on a hot path (workers are long-lived).
958		for i := range out {
959			if out[i].PID > 0 {
960				alive := isPIDAlive(out[i].PID)
961				out[i].PIDAlive = &alive
962			}
963		}
964	
965		return out, nil
966	}

979	// Stop performs a graceful termination of the worker:
980	//  1. Mark state=stopping and persist so observers see the transition.
981	//  2. Emit bead.stopped event + release the bead claim (if one is held).
982	//  3. Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group; escalate to SIGKILL
983	//     after WatchdogKillGrace if the leader is still alive. Pure-goroutine
984	//     workers have no PID — ctx cancellation below is the only lever.
985	//  4. Cancel the worker's context so the loop and in-flight executor exit.
986	//  5. Mark state=stopped and persist. runWorker preserves this terminal
987	//     state when it writes its final record.
988	//
989	// Stop is idempotent: a second call is a no-op. It returns an error only
990	// when the worker is unknown to the manager (already exited / never existed).
991	// stopStaleDiskEntry handles the case where a worker's disk record shows
992	// state=running but no live goroutine exists (e.g. after a server restart).
993	// It releases any held bead claim and flips the on-disk state to "stopped".
994	func (m *WorkerManager) stopStaleDiskEntry(id string) error {
995		dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
996		rec, err := m.readRecord(dir)
997		if err != nil {
998			return fmt.Errorf("worker not running")
999		}
1000		// Terminal state — nothing left to clean up.
1001		if rec.State != "running" && rec.State != "stopping" {
1002			return nil
1003		}
1004	
1005		now := time.Now().UTC()
1006		beadID := ""
1007		if rec.CurrentAttempt != nil {
1008			beadID = rec.CurrentAttempt.BeadID
1009		}
1010		if beadID == "" {
1011			beadID = rec.CurrentBead
1012		}
1013		projectRoot := rec.ProjectRoot
1014		if projectRoot == "" {
1015			projectRoot = m.projectRoot
1016		}
1017	
1018		if beadID != "" {
1019			store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1020			body := fmt.Sprintf(
1021				"worker=%s pid=%d reason=stop-stale",
1022				id, rec.PID,
1023			)
1024			_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1025				Kind:      "bead.stopped",
1026				Summary:   "stop (stale)",
1027				Body:      body,
1028				Actor:     "ddx",
1029				Source:    "server-workers",
1030				CreatedAt: now,
1031			})
1032			_ = store.Unclaim(beadID)
1033		}
1034	
1035		rec.State = "stopped"
1036		rec.Status = "stopped"
1037		if rec.FinishedAt.IsZero() {
1038			rec.FinishedAt = now
1039		}
1040		rec.Lifecycle = append(rec.Lifecycle, WorkerLifecycleEvent{
1041			Action:    "stop",
1042			Actor:     "local-operator",
1043			Timestamp: now,
1044			Detail:    fmt.Sprintf("reason=stop-stale pid=%d", rec.PID),
1045			BeadID:    beadID,
1046		})
1047		return m.writeRecord(dir, rec)
1048	}

1050	func (m *WorkerManager) Stop(id string) error {
1051		m.mu.Lock()
1052		handle := m.workers[id]
1053		if handle == nil || handle.cancel == nil {
1054			m.mu.Unlock()
1055			// Not in-memory: try to clean up a stale disk record left by a
1056			// previous server run or an abruptly killed goroutine.
1057			return m.stopStaleDiskEntry(id)
1058		}
1059		if handle.stopped {
1060			m.mu.Unlock()
1061			return nil
1062		}
1063		handle.stopped = true
1064	
1065		now := time.Now().UTC()
1066		projectRoot := handle.record.ProjectRoot
1067		if projectRoot == "" {
1068			projectRoot = m.projectRoot
1069		}
1070		pid := handle.record.PID
1071		beadID := ""
1072		if handle.record.CurrentAttempt != nil {
1073			beadID = handle.record.CurrentAttempt.BeadID
1074		}
1075		if beadID == "" {
1076			beadID = handle.record.CurrentBead
1077		}
1078		startedAt := handle.record.StartedAt
1079		handle.record.State = "stopping"
1080		handle.record.Status = "stopping"
1081		handle.record.Lifecycle = append(handle.record.Lifecycle, WorkerLifecycleEvent{
1082			Action:    "stop",
1083			Actor:     "local-operator",
1084			Timestamp: now,
1085			Detail:    fmt.Sprintf("reason=stop pid=%d", pid),
1086			BeadID:    beadID,
1087		})
1088		dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
1089		transitionSnapshot := handle.record
1090		cancel := handle.cancel
1091		m.mu.Unlock()
1092	
1093		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, transitionSnapshot)
1094	
1095		// Release the bead claim first — this is durable and must not be
1096		// leaked even if the SIGKILL path blocks for the full grace window.
1097		if beadID != "" {
1098			store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1099			runtime := time.Duration(0)
1100			if !startedAt.IsZero() {
1101				runtime = now.Sub(startedAt)
1102			}
1103			body := fmt.Sprintf(
1104				"worker=%s runtime=%s pid=%d reason=stop",
1105				id, runtime.Round(time.Second), pid,
1106			)
1107			_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1108				Kind:      "bead.stopped",
1109				Summary:   "stop",
1110				Body:      body,
1111				Actor:     "ddx",
1112				Source:    "server-workers",
1113				CreatedAt: now,
1114			})
1115			_ = store.Unclaim(beadID)
1116		}
1117	
1118		// Escalate to the process group if we know the PID.
1119		_, _, _, grace := m.watchdogDeadlines()
1120		if pid > 0 {
1121			terminateProcessGroup(pid, grace)
1122		}
1123	
1124		// Cancel the worker goroutine so any in-process code sees context.Canceled.
1125		cancel()
1126	
1127		// Flip in-memory state to the terminal "stopped" label. For real
1128		// workers, runWorker's final writeRecord (with preservedState) will
1129		// persist this to disk — we deliberately do not writeRecord here a
1130		// second time because runWorker may still be mid-finalization and a
1131		// double-write races the test cleanup. Idle handles (no runWorker)
1132		// have their state observable in-memory; callers that need disk
1133		// persistence for those can call writeRecord directly.
1134		m.mu.Lock()
1135		handle.record.State = "stopped"
1136		handle.record.Status = "stopped"
1137		// Only stamp FinishedAt for handles with no attached runWorker
1138		// goroutine (logFile is the tell — StartExecuteLoop always sets it).
1139		// For real workers, runWorker sets FinishedAt after its own cleanup.
1140		if handle.logFile == nil && handle.record.FinishedAt.IsZero() {
1141			handle.record.FinishedAt = time.Now().UTC()

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	By default work submits to the running ddx server as a background worker and
37	returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current process.
38	`,
39			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
40	  ddx work
41	
42	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
43	  ddx work --local --once
44	
45	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
46	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
47	
48	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
49	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
50	
51	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
52	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
53			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
54			RunE: f.runWork,
55		}
56	
57		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
58		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
59		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
60		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
61		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
63		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
64		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
65		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
66		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
67		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
68		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
69		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Run inline in current process instead of server worker (default: submit to server)")
71		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")
72		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
74		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
75		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
76		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
77		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
78		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
79		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
80	
81		return cmd
82	}

84	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
85	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
86	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
87	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
88	// by this layer.
89	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
90		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true)
91	}
